Charlie was referred back to us by lealand Faulding at Lean Rocket Lab. Leland help him secure SBIR Grants for his new products. He has developed products using an electric screen that generates heat. The screen can be attached to wall panels, ceiling panels and carpet. He will start by selling on Amazon or Home depot. He has experience with both platforms but he would like to sell to hospitals where they could have direct heat instead of using warmed blankets.


He found another individual doing the same thing and they will be working together. He seeks assistance on an operating agreement.


He doesn't need funding at this time, but if his business grows he may need additional working capital. He currently banks with Lake Trust.

Long call with Kasey about her business. She worked on the forensics I had recommended and found a lot of things, like she wasn't charging for shipping, she was paying for technology and services she wasn't using. She is becoming leaner. She also is raising her prices. She sounds like she has a hard time charging people. People who tell her she is expensive make her feel guilty. We talked about it being okay to raise prices, and make money because she has to pay her bills. Her prices are too low, But she also hasn't looked at her competitors to know what they are charging. She offers good value to local people in the Kalkaska area. She needs to let them know her value and remind them that they are working with a person, not a bot and a sweatshop. She is raising her prices July 1. We are speaking again in late July .


She said she appreciates having someone to talk to about her business. She asked about AI and I sent her the AI guide for small businesses.
Reached out to Dequontay to see if he needs any assistance.

Simon Yevzelman

Good afternoon, Andrew! It was great meeting you yesterday and getting to know you and your business venture. Since you're looking to purchase the business and develop a plan for funding, you'll need to gather the following as this is what the lender will want to see:

  • 3 years of tax returns (both for the business, the business owner personally and yourself) - you can redact the EIN or SSN for confidentiality reasons 
  • 3 years of historical and audited financial statements from the seller (that have been reviewed by an accountant)
  • Financial projections for when you own and operate the business (which we can build together)

You may also want to reach back out to the lender for the checklist of items they'll want from you since this may not be an exhaustive list.

I’ve attached a business plan template to get started, please fill it out to the best of your ability. I’ll be here to guide yo

Summary


Julie entered figures into profit cents and valzy



When you run the reports- you should see accrual versus checking cash.


I googled this. There are videos out there as well.

To run an accrual report in QuickBooks, you'll need to

navigate to the desired report, select the "Accrual" accounting method, and then run the report

. You can typically find the accounting method selection within the report's customization options or under the report's settings. 


Detailed Steps:

  1. Open QuickBooks and navigate to the Reports section. 

  3. Choose the report you want to view on an accrual basis. This could be a Profit and Loss, Balance Sheet, or any other report you need. 

  5. Locate the "Accounting Method&
The request is now complete.

Meeting Resources. Sent email


It was a pleasure meeting with you and discussing your business. Here are the resources I mentioned. 


Business Plan: https://www.score.org/sites/default/files/2023-02/SCORE-Startup-Business-Plan-Template.docx


Cash Flow By Month: https://michigansbdc.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/06/Cash-Flow-Projection-and-Variance-Analysis-Spreadsheet.xlsx


Guide: https://michigansbdc.org/wp-content/uploads/2021/10/MI-SBDC_FullBook_Updated-Digi

Daniel and I went over the financials for John. Based on these, it will be a challenge for John to get additional bank financing. After some probing questions from Daniel, a major concern is the lack of marketing. John is currently the only one handling any marketing. So marketing needs to be prioritized. This includes:

  • Hiring a sales manager
  • More attention to consistent promotional postings on Facebook and Instagram
  • Allocate more money to advertising. The current advertising-to-sales ratio is well below industry standards.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: We discussed an update on the company status, and examined the current prototype wand device. She has a pilot going at a Chicago area school and will be collecting anonymized data. Discussed a potential compelling funding outreach if a future user would consent to be a poster child through a human interest approach, beyond raw data collection. Also discussed modular swappable handles with different branding possibilities. The current prototype (one of two built) is now remarkably light weight, and will improve on durability and ergonomics with future iterations. We will explore AI applications for the product in future discussions. She is having staffing issues, and we discussed trade offs and opportunities to address that.

Finally met with Darien. Last year, they had $126,000 in revenue with $15,000-$20,000 revenue per vehicle that they outfitted with a profit of 8-10%. The challenge they face is that the long time it takes to receive payment is causing cash flow problems. Their need is a line of credit of about $25,000. The current bank is Miner's State Bank. The main issue will be collateral.

The next step is to meet with Joanne at MSB.

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: SBDC Checking In: Your Business Progress Update

Made final revisions to business plan based on conversations with Jenessa and Daniel. Also spoke with Karyn Iskra and SNB. Fowarded business plan to Jenessa:


Hi Jenessa,


Here is an updated business plan in Word Format. I tried to make most of the changes we discussed yesterday. Please go over and make any necessary corrections.


I spoke with Karyn at SNB. As you prepare to meet with the bank, keep the following in mind:


1. They typically will not give more than the purchase price of the business. So the financial portion of the business plan includes a loan for $180,000. To get a loan for $200,000, you would need to show where the money would be used, such as building improvements, etc.

2. Also, they typically will only finance 80%. Which means you will typically need to come up with the other 20%, or $36,000

3. If you can come up with $18,000, that leaves you with another $18,000 to raise. The bank shou

Facebook- Tammy will be graduating from her schooling soon and she will need to have her spiritual guide/holistic items business up and running. She was afraid of being a business so she ignored the teachings so now we're trying to get her back up to speed ready to open a spiritual leadership business.


today we focused on how Facebook is a tool to help promote her business. She never had a personal profile so we're starting from scratch. She did the set up in March but hasn't done anything since. We talked about friend requesting people she knows and then inviting them to her business profile, today she added two people.


We talked about Canva and creating content so potential customers have something to look at on her Facebook page but we are going to wait as Tammy is focused on finishing her schooling.

I’m trying to take it one day at a time and realizing I have a few options out there….I want to revisit training/certification on QBO and see if getting the “points” to have a referral profile pans out. Otherwise, I’ve heard a couple times from Diane if she can pass on my information and my one CPA referral contact seems like a good source. If I serve my newest client well he’ll also be a good referral resource.

Email/response


That is great!


I am not aware of any promo codes, but if I find any I will send along. 


Can you answer the questions below? I want to tell our funders the good news!


SBA has asked that we collect some data on your experience. We would love to know if you started your business since we last spoke. If so, could you reply to this email with the following information?

 

Start Up Business

Name of Business:

Have you registered your business (LLC, S Corp):

Month and year:

 

Have you had any revenue from operating the business?

Amount:

Through This Date/Time Period:

 

Have you acquired debt or received a grant to purchase inventory, equipment, building, etc.?

Type (credit card, bank loan, line of credit, grant, etc.):

D: Client wants to buy an existing machine shop


ACP: Buy resources


R/A:

  1. Need 3 years tax returns. For the last few years, he has voluntarily cut back. Will get 5 years
  2. BP
  3. Training
  4. 5C: credit score mid-600 (low), both client and wife will still work, understands 10-30%, has $100k in equity in home. Business is a going concern. May need home equity or owner financing due to credit score
  5. Need to get attorney and ask about buying biz vs buying assets. Can wait to file LLC until he gets this answer

NS:

  1. Client will review f/u email
  2. Client will send tax returns
  3. Client will send BP

AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: She needs funds to buy a business

 

RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN: Jacob and I worked to develop the latest version of Christie's opening day balance sheet and projections. We also wrote up a one-pager explaining each of the line items so that Choice One has the rationalization for each one. We will be in contact with Christie to make any adjustments necessary.

 

NEXT STEPS & FOLLOW-UP ACTIONS: She will present these documents to the bank

I think the 15-minute sit-and-color station is a good idea, especially with the late event time. A $5–10 fee or donation box sounds good. Framing it as an adult-friendly, relaxing activity could help attract more adults.

I like the community bear idea with the raffle entry. Offering discounts for future workshops and a demo at the table would also be strong draws.

Glad to hear about Fiddleleaf Café, sounds like a great fit for the photo tiles. Hope it works out!

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Phone call from client

David Darbeshire contacted B. Gettel to discuss the Vfw building he purchased in Pinconning to convert into Co-working space. His intention is to target rural communities and provide economic assistance and provide a business incubator similar to CMURC. He indicated he is on the MI Works board at the State level and is located in Oakland County. He said he would like to ID the entrepreneurs in the community and find the best way to reach them. Indicated Michelle J. is his normal consultant and he would like to build awareness of his facility and find the best way to reach out in the community. The VFW is in a residential neighborhood.


He provided a long list of people he has spoke to in the EDC areas including Erin Strang and Jim R. at Bay Future. Hi Rickey, 


Glad you began the process of evaluating your options and considered the work necessary to launch a successful business. You will need to work closely with the SpeedQueen franchise department. Find an attorney to review the Franchise agreement before signing.

Do you have to find your own space, do they have the criteria written out to evaluate the feasibility of the space? 

Generally speaking for financing purposes you will need about 20% down, owner equity on a loan. Try to keep your personal financial situation as clean as possible.

You need a written business plan with 3 years financial projections. The most important section of the business plan are those financial projections so we should start with that. I have attached a template to look over, it's the one my clients typically use. Let me know your availability for a phone or Zoom meeting and we can go from there.


Diana

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Finalized the starting point tab on the financial projections as well as added the new proposed costs for the additional 12 bed AFC. We looked up their current legal structures in LARA and discussed what they will call the new 12 bed AFC and Center. We titled the spreadsheet the proposed name The Center LLC, where both AFC's will rent space from The Center. The total project costs for relocating the 6 bed AFC to the new location and adding 6 beds was $758,592 with $104,377 Owner Investment, $587,213 commercial loan and $67,002 credit card debt. The total refinancing and new debt of the additional 12 beds will be $1,328, 326.


We discussed the rates from USDA Business & Industry Loan Guarantee and SBA 504 loan programs. We projected 7% with a payback in 30 years. The total for this is close to what they are currently paying in monthly payments with only the one 12 bed facility.


Plan: We will meet on Thursday, June 12th from 10-11:30am.

Re: video shoot update

Thanks Dione,


This is interesting and fun! And ambitious, especially considering the disruptions at NEA and beyond.  


I understand the artistic draw of projects like this and the trains, and I believe there is value in having a full pipeline of future projects in various stages of readiness. However, as your business advisor, I have to mention that these seem to me like distractions from more urgent work. To the extent that you're wanting to share more about you and your work, and are looking for feedback on potential future projects, I'm happy to provide my input. To the extent that you're trying to get traction for your art on clothing, I have to encourage you to focus more on that.  


Good luck with your marketing meeting this week! And, is your meeting with Rich Sheridan on the 30th in person, or virtual? As always, let me know if you want to talk again, before or after either of those.  


Talk to you

My Reply: 6/9/2025 11:06 AM

Hi Tantanisha,

If you’re looking for local government websites where you can find contracting opportunities (like janitorial bids), I recommend reaching out to ChatGPT for a quick list. It can pull up the current portals and links for Kalamazoo-area governments like the City of Kalamazoo, Kalamazoo County, and nearby cities like Portage — all in one place.

Just ask: “Can you give me a list of local government websites that post janitorial contracting opportunities in the Kalamazoo area?”

It’ll get you what you need fast — and I’m still happy to help with reviewing any proposals or registrations once you’re ready.

As to how to do the bids, most of the websites I've looked at have instructions of how they want them submitted. You'll most like

ClientIntake.CompanyName: Cathe's Lodging LLC DBA L'Anse Motel

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes: Met up with Cathe at the SBSH grant writing event in Baraga. Checked in on her progress. Biggest needs now are online booking and modernization for room locking, wants to go to coded doors instead of key doors. Just broke her foot, so she is dealing with that. She is meeting with Robin Chosa and hopes to apply for direct grant to update locks on doors. Also needs money for some property improvements, suggested that we do a cash flow forecast to plan out a loan, since grants are probably not forthcoming for deferred maintenance. Assigned file to Kevin for monitoring going forward, as he also attended the event.

I met with Kolmarge at the Morey Tech Center to discuss his new potential lending contact. He's been in contact with Paul Scoby of Isabella Bank, who needs updated documentation to consider Kolmarge for a small loan.

I asked Kolmarge how much he was looking for, and what it would be used for. At first, he was looking for $50,000 with no set plans, but then we figured out that he would likely need around $21,000 for the competition ring, updated marketing and web build, and working capital. They will be moving to a new space in Centennial Hall, where the Isabella County Restoration House used to be.

The new space is much nicer, and has a kitchenette and a shower facility for clients in training. He also got a good deal, of only $4,000 in rent for the next 6 months.

We went through all of the documentation that Kolmarge would need, and we worked more on the business plan pitch. He provided updated tax returns for 2024 to help me tweak financial projections. </

Hi Ivan,

Thanks again for meeting with me last week. It was great to hear that you’ve already priced the ice machine ($200) and connected with Jessica K. at the county office about your plan review, STFU licensing, and other requirements.

A few resources to keep you on track:

  1. Plan-Review Checklist – make sure the layout, plumbing, and equipment list match what you’ll submit.
  2. STFU & Mobile Food License Guide – double-check water-tank capacity, waste-water disposal, and on-board hand-wash station specs.
  3. Written Procedures – draft simple SOPs (food sourcing, holding temps, cleaning schedule); the county will ask for them.
  4. ServSafe Manager Certification – not legally required for STFU, but highly recommended and often speeds approval.

Let me know if you need sample SOPs, commissary options, or financing contacts for equipment. I’m happy to help.

If you

90 Day Business Refinement Cohort

Hello James and Helen!


I apologize for the delay in getting this email to you. I was on the road today and a client with an urgent and unexpected need came up. I just got home, so I'm sitting down to get you the information I promised.


The purpose of the program is to help legitimize side-hustle operations and prepare small business owners to expand activity beyond the borders of their friends and family circle. CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
1:1 business planning session, prep, BARN notes


B - On top of what is happening in the world of technology in real time. His idea is to be the conduit between AI Agents (created by Software Engineers and Developers) and Enterprise (businesses who want to automate more). His company will have a leader board of prospective Agents who can best solve their stated problem. Like Indeed for AI.


A - Research which industries are in the best position to take advantage of his solution. Cautioned Mohan that he no spend too much time developing a solution until he knows what the problem is. Find industries that are already on board as they may be more receptive to his solution if it's better and faster, as opposed

Reviewed financial forecast and made updates during conversation. Key points are to watch profitability and positive cash flow. Biggest opportunity to improve are to increase subscription number/prices and reduce owner draws. Suggested maybe taking a loan instead of soliciting grant due to tax implications in first year, but with accelerated depreciation may not be an issue. He will let me know when he wants to meet again. His goals this week: meet with Osceola Twp about potential lease, talking to more potential grant funders, get a survey out to get data about consumer interest and willingness to pay prices he is estimating.

I met with Tom to discuss the SEO Audit of the website, now that the GLBBH grant applications are over.

We went through the basic rubric, and discussed where he was doing well - his keywords are really on point, and he has great internal and external linking.

Tom could use some more work on page titles and meta descriptions. Because he has hundreds of products with individual pages, it is no wonder that he may need some work on these. I advised him to do what he could to update titles and descriptions, but to not include information that could be misleading just for the sake of meeting the recommended length.

He asked about any paid resources that might be able to do more intel on his competition, but I am not aware of any at this time. He does seem to be outstripping his competitors in keywords, so that's a good thing.

It was enlightening to see the back end of Tom's site as we discussed. We went through some more mechanics of what should go where

Good afternoon Kevin! That’s such exciting news, congrats on moving forward with the space and the LOI! It sounds like things are really starting to take shape, and I’m glad to hear Rob’s been a great resource so far.

As for how the SBDC can support you going forward, I’d be happy to jump in where needed. I'm happy to guide in any and all areas of starting and running your business. Operationally, marketing, financially, etc. Based on where you're at, I could see assist in: 

  • Financial projections and cash flow planning
  • Business planning for lender readiness
  • Marketing strategy, branding, or customer acquisition planning
  • Operational workflows as you prepare to open

If there are specific questions or areas you're feeling unsure about, feel free to send them my way, or we can schedule a check-in to talk through everything and map out how I can best support the next phase.

Looking forward to hearing more and helping h

Has made a little progress with written plan. We still need the 2025 actual numbers and the Sources/Uses of funds. Client will work on this in advance of 6/17 meeting and send my way for review prior to the session. We revisited the pricing discussion and I still believe the $75/$125 to be a good mix between the 45-minute and 90-minute sessions. Discussed add-ons and what that may mean - bundle vs add-on. I prefer the bundled approach except perhaps for aromatherapy as an add-on as some people are very sensitive to smell. (client includes it) This may become a new add-on. Considering.

Client also mentioned that her realtor suggested setting up a separate LLC for the property when they buy it. I agree - may want to sell the business and keep the building as a landlord to earn some passive income. (people do this all the time) We may do the LLC together in the next session as well.

Meeting with Jesse Schultz

Description:


I met with Jesse Schultz and Colleen to discuss Jesse's plans for opening a Kids United franchise in Lansing, Michigan, which focuses on child development through soccer for children aged 18 months to 9 years. I explained that his consulting services, funded by the Small Business Administration, are available at no cost to assist small businesses. He outlined the support offered, including help with starting, buying, or expanding businesses and securing financial capital. Jesse detailed the funding requirements for the franchise, totaling around $470,000, and mentioned his outreach to lenders, including assistance from Cam Abood at MSU FCU, while expressing concerns about Lake Trust's support.


The discussion included financing options, with Me emphasizing the importance of realistic financial projections and having a selected facility to provide lenders with a cl

I spoke with Joe by telephone seeking a status update. Times are slow for his business. He laid off all his employees for the summer and is waiting for the students to return in August. He continues to try out different ideas for promoting his business. He is trying to get back in as a vendor for the sports teams as they order food quite often for the players.


Lower revenues this past year has impacted his personal credit. He was behind $11k at the start of the year and now owes $7k - making some progress. In all it sounds fairly grim but he intends to hang in there.

Okay, I can try reaching out to get a checklist for you from Jessica. What percentage of the papers do you think you have completed that she gave you? Reviewed PR firm's terms agreement and had a conversation in which I suggested that any mention of withholding IP in a technology firm agreement is just bad business practice. I said that I understood they were using that as a means to enforce collections, but really!


The agency explained it was only meant to refer to creatives the agency developed. Given the nature of the engagement with the client, they agreed to waive the terms, and to reconsider in the future. Client was ok with the results.

Worked on creating Amanda's business plan and developing her financial projections. Reviewed projections with Harry before sending them off to Amanda. I will await her feedback and make any adjustments necessary.

Agrability Problem


I spoke with Michael and Shana about their issues with the program Agrability. Michael and Shana have some financial needs for renovating their business space for Michael as he is a disabled Veteran. Michael has been given a quote regarding what it would cost to complete these adjustsments through Agrability and they explained that in the past they have worked with Veterans Affairs office to finance the renovations. The issue that is presented Agrability cannot find their contact that they have worked with in the past for this program and no one at the VA office is aware of Agrability or any program like that.


I offered to make contacts on their behalf and see if I can gain insights into this issue. I have sent them the consent agreement and will begin making calls as soon as I get the consent back.

Discussed funding needs and business plan. Client provided both -- provided feedback on business plan document and developed detailed and summary level financials which were shared with client.

Email/response


Answering your questions. 


Marketing

Were you able to view the workshops? As we discussed, identifying which businesses ae looking for your talent will be the most important thing for you to discover. 


Ideal/Target Market

Defining your target market is all about identifying your Ideal Customers – that group of buyers who perceive the most value in your product offering (and are willing and able to pay for that value). By identifying and targeting specific market segments, you’ll be able to focus your resources on attracting the most profitable customers. This doesn’t necessarily mean you’re going to exclude segments that don’t fit your “ideal” criteria. You’re just not going to spend a lot of time or money marketing directly to them. See http://smallbusiness.chron.com/identif

Christie faced several challenges as we reviewed her health tech startup's financial forecasts and staffing model. Her original sales projections were too high given the product development timeline, which won’t complete until mid-2026. We found discrepancies in her customer and navigator calculations, as well as unrealistic assumptions about how many accounts each sales or client services rep could manage. Additionally, the existing sales staffing model didn’t align well with Ted’s more conservative estimates, particularly when it came to conversion rates, sales cycles, and overall sales capacity. These factors contributed to a negative operating income and underscored the need to revisit her revenue, R&D, and staffing assumptions.


I recommended removing revenue projections for 2025 entirely, since there won’t be a sellable product until late 2026. We discussed splitting the sales team into lead generation reps and sales development reps, each with specific
Reviewing requested potential customer list and sent to client.

Summary/Action: I wrote the client that I have looked over the pitch that they sent and I believe that there are two main factors that didn’t pass the NSF invite parameters: NSF has new priorities because of the incoming administration. Right or wrong, there is now an increasing emphasis on making sure that technologies are not focused toward a certain group (whether that be based on sex, race, religion, socio-economic class, or other groups) and instead have a broad impact. Information can be found here. I believe that they will need to eliminate their focus on “underrepresented”, “low-income”, “minority” groups and take out the benefits to “affluent” groups. What they are saying is certainly real, but, unfortunately, in this political climate, it is going against the new priorities that NSF is having to administer to. And whatever they do, work on bias, but don’t say that they are working on bias. That is not something that is accepted right now. Instead is there a way to focus th

Summary/Action: I wrote to the client that it was nice to speak with them yesterday. Per our conversation, attached they will find: NSF: The NSF Project Pitch template. Remember that this is required as they MUST get an invitation to apply from the NSF before they can submit a full proposal. This can take up to 4 weeks to get a reply but usually takes about 1 week. The NSF topic areas (which they will need to submit their project pitch) are found here. Remember that these areas are fixed but the subtopics are non-exhaustive and they will see at the end of each topic an “other” subtopic. If there is a subtopic that matches their technology, they should use that category, but if not, they would still like to look at it under the other category. The NSF Phase I full proposal template to give they an idea of what they need to prepare for when they get the pitch invitation. It may also help they start thinking through their project plan and refine what they want to put into their pitch.

[*HRS are cumulative (Uplift/TRYE Team)]


AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

David requested a one-on-one consultation to provide updates on his recent business progress and seek guidance on several matters. He has proactively enrolled in an eight-week small business tax workshop offered by the IRS in partnership with the Small Business Support Hub programming and the Latinx Technology and Community Center. This educational opportunity is helping him understand critical business concepts including expenses, deductions, exemptions, and proper record keeping for tax purposes. Additionally, David has received some documents in the mail that he doesn't understand and needs assistance interpreting their content and implications for his business.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

We completed the final review and submission of David's $10,000 grant proposal during our call, which represents a significant milestone in his fundin

Share research with Justin


Justin brought in samples of his product and demonstrated how it is made.

Walked Justin through the folder of research I gathered and related the findings to his position.

Much of the research is tobacco-focused, but some is cannabis.

Any of it can be extrapolated to his situation

The margins seem to be low, rate of smoking is decreasing and tariffs are a big concern

Also recommended that he consider a very small footrpint, as overhead will affect net profit

Industry returns are 2-4.9 percent, which does not leave much room for error

Justin was extremely pleased with the amount of research and will review it


Justin will reach out schedule our next meeting

I met with Shana and Curtis at Ballyhoo Books & Brew to introduce myself and get to know a little bit more about their business. One of their main concerns right now is consolidating ZENlightenment and RedBloom. At the moment, Shana is mainly doing her RedBloom work remotely, and the space in St. Louis is largely used as storage. It would be helpful to keep both ventures under one umbrella for simplification purposes:

  • Only one book of business to keep track of
  • Decreased insurance costs for one entity instead of two
  • Smaller footprint - operating out of one location rather than two

They asked if it would be possible for ZEN to "buy" RedBloom, and if so, would a purchase agreement need to be made? Would Shana pay herself for the purchase? To this end, I assume that they would need to speak with their CPA and lawyer to make sure that this would be allowable and would be the best course of action.

My Response to Marc:


Hi Marc,


Yes, if you have the EIN number you can get a business bank account. 


If you have got your LLC already, there are a couple things to look out for. Since the LLC information is public information scammers can find your information and will try to get money from you.


You'll likely receive 3 "scam" items now that you've filed your LLC - which you can put on pause for now

  1. A company wanting to file your BOI (Beneficial Owner

Decided to move on to reconcile her Checking account. Went over the statement, tried to match transactions and realized she sent the 2024 statement. Waiting for the right statement.

Aaron called to discuss break even point if buys the new bus. It will cost $700/mo for the loan, and $2000/mo for the insurance. It will only be insured for 6 months. He will charge $700 per 4 our tour, and $150 per additional hour. We did not not consider oil changes and maintenance. But, he needs 5 tours per month or 30 tours per season to cover these costs. He also needs to get workers compensation insurance. He's going to speak with his agent. He's waiting on the insurance quotes to come back from the agency on the bus before he can buy it. Somehow the state is responsible for this? I didn't understand that at the end. Nevertheless, he is holding and will contact me when it comes through. Potential Impact.

Media.Other: I found your website on a search engine

CounselorRecord.CounselorNotes:
Alyssa was on the call today. Chris had an issue. She said they want to get people through the door. She said the roll-out has not been what they expected, but they have 6 people booked this week (it's growing). They have some repeat customers. They have the billboard launching today. She said she needs to plan the social media and actually do the posts. It sounds like running a small business is work and they want other people to do it. The owner is not the operator. I emailed Stacey Sanford's information to them. She's glad to have someone who can potentially help them.


The website looks better, not perfect. I suggested some kinds of promotion to get some new customers in the door, some incentive since repeat customers will make this business really happen. She didn't love the idea of the discout, but maybe a % off for the 4th. She said they did a Fathers Day sale. So why not the 4th?


I'll call Chris next Wednesday.

Introductory conversation. They are up against a fast track NSF deadline of July 2nd for an STTR and so will be focusing on completing the application. I have connected them to Anna Bier for a draft review. The company has been through regional and national ICorp, so they are expected to have a pretty well developed business canvas. Dr. Khalid stated that on SOTA benchmarks they are 15% ahead, and while this is not a huge differentiator, it may give them the edge as being a premier provider, especially in the legal market, permitting their clients to assert that they use the best fake detection software available, etc. It is a fast moving industry, so they will have to execute quickly to stay on top, even with a Neuro Symbolic mixture of experts approach. They will send a data room link to review and look for any gaps in their planning, and to assess their readiness for connecting them to additional funding resources.

[*HRS are cumulative (Uplift/TRYE Team)]


AN ANALYSIS OF THE CRITICAL PROBLEM:

Annette reached out requesting assistance with obtaining her food service permit, specifically asking about "Flint Food Works" for the certification process. She expressed interest in serving food through pop-ups and eventually expanding into a larger food business, with plans to start small and grow organically. 


Additionally, Annette mentioned wanting to develop and sell healthy seasonings as a complementary business venture. She indicated potential relocation plans by the end of the year, either to Detroit or Ann Arbor to be closer to her daughters, which could impact where she pursues her food service certification. The timing was fortuitous as there was a ServSafe class scheduled for that very day that had been rescheduled, presenting a potential immediate opportunity.


RECOMMENDATIONS AND ACTIONS TO BE TAKEN:

ServSafe certificati

Hi Percy,

Thank you for your patience while I work through a busy client schedule. It was great meeting with you last week and learning more about your prosthetic leaf-rake project. You’re on an inspiring path!

What we covered

  • You have a functional prototype and are refining it with your associate engineer (via VBOC).
  • Your target market includes one-arm users and disabled veterans.
  • Your immediate goal is a marketable prototype to show investors and begin the patent process.
  • You asked about small-batch manufacturing options.

Next steps & resources

  1. Prototype refinement – Continue working with your engineer to finalize design tweaks.
  2. Manufacturing leads – I’m reaching out to a few contacts who specialize in low-volume plastic and metal fabrication. I’ll share those names as soon as I hear back.
  3. Patent prep – As yo

There is some positive news following our recent ice storm disaster.


Gretchen Whitmer announced that the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA) has granted an administrative disaster declaration for Cheboygan County and the contiguous counties of Charlevoix, Emmet, Mackinac, Montmorency, Otsego, and Presque Isle, which unlocks financial assistance to repair or replace property damaged by the historic ice storms.

Hi Clay,

Thank you for reaching out. It has been a while since our last communication.

To give you a quick update, the last couple of months have been us winning a grant from ann arbor spark to build one of our solar trailers here in the US for showcase. For April and May that unit was deployed at the Container Globe outdoor theatre to help power their art installation for over two weeks. 


Further we had our first two customer deliveries, in India for farmers in rural Maharashtra. 

The silence from my side is purely due to burning the candle at both ends between the US development as well as global.


I am now in a place where I am looking at applications to SBIR and other resources that i can tap into as we have developed a unit to showcase. 

I
